commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR expands plugin metadata snapshot memoization to an 8-entry persisted/provided LRU and threads metadata snapshots/discovery through provider and auto-enable hot paths to reduce TUI/gateway cold-start plugin work.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: PR head loads a metadata snapshot with `params.workspaceDir`, then computes the active registry workspace later in `resolvePluginProviderLoadBase`. I did not rerun the cold-start profiler in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can align snapshot workspace resolution with existing active-registry workspace inheritance and add focused regression coverage.

target.title: perf(plugins): cut plugin-system thrash from TUI cold start
target.body: Improvement to plugin loading performance on gateway and TUI cold start. BEFORE AFTER I ran this a few times and reliably just a second or 3 less ## Summary Cuts duplicated plugin-system work during plugin resolve / cold start. Three classes of fix, ranked by impact: ### 1. Plumbing — `PluginMetadataSnapshot` threaded through resolve paths (~2–4s) `resolvePluginProviders`, `isPluginProvidersLoadInFlight`, `resolveOwningPluginIdsForProvider`, and `resolveExternalAuthProfilesWithPlugins` all dispatch to helpers that have a `manifestRegistry` fast path. They now thread the snapshot through so the fast path actually fires instead of every helper rebuilding the manifest registry from disk. `loadPluginManifestRegistryForInstalledIndex` fires drop ~685 → ~10 per cold start. A second plumb threads `discovery?: PluginDiscoveryResult` through `applyPluginAutoEnable` → `detectPluginAutoEnableCandidates` → `resolvePluginAutoEnableReadiness` → `collectConfiguredChannelIds` → the bundled-channel leaf helpers, so the caller's already-resolved discovery short-circuits the cascade. This is the load-bearing perf fix and is independent of memoization — it works because we don't redundantly call `loadPluginMetadataSnapshot` within a single resolve chain. ### 2. Cache-busting — snapshot memo store/lookup keys now match (~7s on persisted path) Previously the memo computed its lookup key from the persisted index and stored entries under the derived index. Those indexes always differ in volatile fields (`generatedAtMs`, `refreshReason`), so the keys never agreed and every lookup missed. **Persisted-path hit rate goes from 24% → ~98%.** After #85201 landed on main, derived snapshots are intentionally excluded from the memo (so newly added plugins under derived discovery paths are discovered without restart). This PR's memo fix applies to the persisted/provided path, which is the steady-state hit path once the persisted registry has been written. ### 3. LRU capacity (supporting) Memo was single-slot and thrashed when two stable input shapes alternated (different `preferPersisted`, different `stateDir`, different config fingerprint). Capacity-8 LRU keeps the realistic set warm. Necessary precondition for the cache-busting fix to land cleanly — without LRU, the memo would still self-evict across distinct memoKeys. Additionally `findFastPathRegistryState` shares the persisted-registry-state computation across entries so a cold memoKey can piggy-back on a sibling entry's fast-path result. ## Setup-CLI-backend owner regression fix [`bd58a706`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/bd58a706) — the rewritten single owner-scan in `resolveOwningPluginIdsForProvider` was missing `plugin.setup?.cliBackends`. Pre-PR the no-registry fallback went through `resolvePluginContributionOwners({ contribution: "cliBackends" })` which includes both top-level and setup CLI backends; the replacement scan needed the same coverage. Includes regression test in `providers.test.ts`. ## Verification - `pnpm tsgo --noEmit --project tsconfig.core.json` — clean - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.memo.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/providers.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/load-context.test.ts` — pass - `pnpm build && pnpm openclaw tui` from a cold process (pre-rebase trace) — freeze 32s → 13–16s, snapshot memo hit rate 24% → ~98% on persisted path, per-plugin `package.json` reads ~914 → ~370 Not run: full `pnpm test` / `pnpm check`, sibling-process plugin-install interleaving. Post-rebase TUI trace pending. ## Coexistence with #85201 #85201 (Vincent Koc, merged today) added `registrySource` to the snapshot and gates `canMemoizePluginMetadataSnapshotResult` on `registrySource !== "derived"` — derived snapshots stay out of the process memo and the runtime current-snapshot handoff. This PR coexists with that boundary: the plumbing/discovery work is unaffected, the LRU + symmetric-key + fast-path-sharing changes apply to persisted/provided memo entries only. ## Follow-on PR An `installRecords` threading optimization that cut ~264 sync disk reads (~140ms freeze) was extracted into a separate PR ([#85026](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85026), already merged).
target.author: romneyda
